commands.repair_reason: - [P2] Needs maintainer review and contributor changes for run-scoped attribution plus API/privacy approval; there is no safe cleanup close or narrow autonomous merge path yet.; Needs attention: The diff introduces a concrete privacy/security-boundary concern because caller-supplied private attribution can persist on session state and be forwarded on later unrelated same-session diagnostic even...; Review finding: [... (sha=cfa89da93b90e4b32a0b25566a39b58dc87924cd)
commands.review_summary:
Adds an optional gateway/SDK `clientContext` request field, normalizes it, stores it on diagnostic session state, and forwards it as `DiagnosticEventPrivateData` to trusted diagnostics listeners, with generated protocol/API baseline updates.

PR surface: Source +240, Tests +339, Generated 0, Other +4. Total +583 across 15 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection gives a high-confidence path: seed `clientContext` for a gateway run, then emit a later same-session `message.queued` or `session.state` through dispatch/lifecycle code that never clears it; the stale private data is still forwarded.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Needs maintainer review and contributor changes for run-scoped attribution plus API/privacy approval; there is no safe cleanup close or narrow autonomous merge path yet.

target.title: feat(gateway): attribute runs via opt-in clientContext on the trusted diagnostic channel
target.body:
## Summary

Lets a gateway `agent` request attach an opaque, plugin-agnostic `clientContext` bag so an **out-of-process observability plugin** can attribute a run's spans to an upstream identity (e.g. an orchestrator/parent task) that it cannot otherwise see. The bag is seeded once onto the run's diagnostic session state and then forwarded to trusted diagnostic observers on every `session.state` / `message.queued` event the run emits ‚Äî the same shared-state path that already carries `sessionKey`.

**`clientContext` rides the trusted `privateData` channel, never the public event payload.** The lifecycle events stay byte-for-byte unchanged for existing `onDiagnosticEvent` subscribers; the bag is delivered only to the trusted diagnostics channel that bundled/official diagnostics exporters already receive through `ctx.internalDiagnostics.onEvent` ‚Äî the same channel the model/tool content-capture path (#91256) uses for its private payloads. No new public subscription API is added: the only plugin-SDK surface change is a `clientContext?` field on the already-exported `DiagnosticEventPrivateData`.

### Why the private channel

`session.state` and `message.queued` are queue/state telemetry. Putting a caller-supplied bag directly on those payloads changes the privacy and cardinality contract for **every** diagnostic subscriber. Instead, the carrying events are still emitted **untrusted** (so they stay visible to `onDiagnosticEvent`, unchanged), while the bag travels in `privateData`. `dispatchDiagnosticEvent` forwards `privateData` to trusted listeners independently of the `trusted` flag, so the private payload reaches the trusted diagnostics grant without ever touching the public stream. Privacy comes from the `privateData` channel, not from flipping the event to trusted (which would make seeded runs vanish from the public stream).

## What changed

- **`src/infra/diagnostic-events.ts`** ‚Äî `clientContext?` added to `DiagnosticEventPrivateData`; new internal `emitInternalDiagnosticEventWithPrivateData` (public/untrusted emit that still carries `privateData` to trusted listeners). No field added to any public event payload.
- **`src/logging/diagnostic.ts`** ‚Äî lifecycle emits branch: a seeded run uses the private emit, an unseeded run uses the plain public emit. Seeding/clearing (`setDiagnosticSessionClientContext`) is unchanged; a reused session whose later run supplies no/invalid context clears the stale bag so it is never misattributed.
- **`src/infra/diagnostic-client-context.ts`** ‚Äî bounded, deep-frozen normalizer: deterministic key ordering, prototype-pollution stripping, whole-bag drop on overflow. The size cap is measured in **UTF-8 bytes** (`Buffer.byteLength`), so multibyte payloads can't slip past a code-unit length check.
- **`src/logging/diagnostic-session-state.ts`** ‚Äî `SessionState.clientContext` stash bridging seed ‚Üí emit.
- **`src/gateway/server-methods/agent.ts`** + **`packages/gateway-protocol/src/schema/agent.ts`** + Swift `GatewayModels` ‚Äî permissive `clientContext` wire field on the agent request (additive; the schema stays `additionalProperties: false`, no protocol version bump).
- **`packages/sdk`** (`types.ts`, `client.ts`) ‚Äî `clientContext?: Record<string, unknown>` on `AgentRunParams`, forwarded verbatim by `runs.create`; the SDK never inspects it.
- **`src/plugin-sdk/diagnostic-runtime.ts`** + `docs/plugins/sdk-subpaths.md` ‚Äî unchanged public subpath surface; API baseline hash regenerated for the new `DiagnosticEventPrivateData.clientContext` field.

## Verification

Verified in an isolated worktree rebased onto current `main` (Node 22.22.1):

- `tsgo -p tsconfig.core.json` ‚Äî clean across all touched files.
- `diagnostic-client-context.test.ts` + `diagnostic-events.test.ts` ‚Äî 28 pass.
- `diagnostic-client-context-events.test.ts` + `diagnostic.test.ts` ‚Äî 73 pass.
- `packages/sdk` `index.test.ts` ‚Äî `clientContext` forwarding test passes.
- `oxlint` + `oxfmt --check` on touched files ‚Äî clean.
- `plugin-sdk:api:gen` + `--check` ‚Äî baseline regenerated, check `OK`.

New tests assert the core invariants directly: a public `onDiagnosticEvent` subscriber receives the lifecycle events **without** any `clientContext` (and `JSON.stringify(publicEvents)` never contains the secret), while the trusted diagnostics channel (`onTrustedInternalDiagnosticEvent`, the same path exposed to exporters via `ctx.internalDiagnostics.onEvent`) receives it via `privateData` on both lifecycle types. The "don't gate on `trusted`" mechanic is covered by a regression test that delivers `privateData` for an *untrusted* emit. The byte-cap test rejects a bag that is under the cap by string length but over it in UTF-8 bytes. The SDK test asserts `clientContext` reaches the agent request verbatim.

## Real behavior proof

Re-captured on the live gateway on 2026-06-10 (~00:05 UTC), driving two back-to-back `agent` runs on the **native Anthropic transport** (`thinker` = `anthropic/claude-opus-4-6`) and reading the resulting OTel spans out of Tempo. The deployed build carries this PR's gateway clientContext-forwarding (commit `ea83b4e20c feat(gateway): forward generic clientContext onto diagnostic events`, equivalent to this branch's `feat(gateway): attribute runs via opt-in clientContext on the trusted diagnostic channel`).

**Behavior addressed:** an out-of-process diagnostics bridge attributes a run's root span to a caller-supplied upstream identity (`Conductor`) delivered over the trusted private channel, with the public lifecycle-event contract unchanged.

**Real environment tested:** live OpenClaw gateway + out-of-process `agentweave-bridge` plugin subscribing to the trusted diagnostics channel; OTel spans exported to Tempo (TraceQL queried directly).

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** drove a seeded run (`sessionKey agent:thinker:seed-‚Ä¶`) whose request carried `clientContext = { schemaVersion: "agentweave.context.v1", source: "conductor", agentId: "Conductor", agentType: "main", taskLabel: ‚Ä¶ }`, then a control run (`sessionKey agent:thinker:ctrl-‚Ä¶`) with **no** `clientContext`. Inspected the bridge log lines and queried Tempo (`{ .prov.agent.id = "Conductor" }` and `{ name = "llm.claude-opus-4-6" }`) over the run window.

**Evidence after fix:**

Bridge logs (A/B):
- Seeded: `started root span for main session: 0df77940-‚Ä¶ agent: Conductor` ‚Üí `ended upstream root span for session: agent:thinker:seed-‚Ä¶`.
- Control: **no** `started root span ‚Ä¶ agent:` line and **no** `ended upstream root span` ‚Äî only `session.state` + `model.call.*`.

Tempo spans (A/B):

| run | trace | span | `prov.agent.id` | `prov.session.id` | link ‚Üí root |
|-----|-------|------|-----------------|-------------------|-------------|
| seeded (root)  | `4a911e7566589ea7f77c05bec440b8a` | `openclaw.turn`        | `Conductor` | ‚Äî | ‚Äî |
| seeded (child) | `593fe6309d2203fd797a3641f5ddcbc4` | `llm.claude-opus-4-6`  | `Conductor` | `0df77940-‚Ä¶` | ‚Üí `4a911e75‚Ä¶` |
| control (child)| `91b10bb1774cd07897b8d3944cc4dfdd` | `llm.claude-opus-4-6`  | `nix-v1` (plugin default) | `nix-main` (default) | none |

**Observed result after fix:** the seeded run's root `openclaw.turn` span is attributed to `Conductor`; the control run produces no upstream root span and falls back to the plugin's default identity (`nix-v1` / `nix-main`). Public `session.state` / `message.queued` payloads carried no `clientContext`.

**What was not tested:** (1) CI has not yet run this exact rebased branch end-to-end against a live Tempo ‚Äî the trace evidence above is from the deployed fork build that carries the same gateway forwarding; this branch's own gates are the worktree type/unit/lint/baseline runs under **Verification**. (2) The **child** LLM-span join (`nix-v1` ‚Üí `Conductor`, seeded child row) additionally depends on an env-gated `x-agentweave-session-key` outbound-request header ‚Äî a **separate fork-local carry, not part of this PR** (and intentionally not upstreamable: hardcoded vendor header name). The behavior **this PR** delivers is the **root-span** attribution (`openclaw.turn` ‚Üí `Conductor`) via clientContext on the trusted diagnostic channel; the child-span row is included only to show the full attribution chain end-to-end. (3) The codex/OAuth transport path is out of scope (deferred to Mux).
target.author: arniesaha
